usage.title: Recipe Usage
usage.noRecipes: This ingredient is not being used in any recipe yet.
usage.noRecipesHint: You can add this ingredient to a product's recipe from the Recipes section.

fieldHelp.sku: Stock Keeping Unit - A unique code to identify and track this ingredient in your inventory system. Example: TOM-001, BEEF-500G
fieldHelp.gtin: Global Trade Item Number - Universal product barcode (UPC/EAN). Used to identify standard commercial products. Example: 7501234567890
fieldHelp.unit: Unit of measurement for this ingredient. This defines how you'll track stock, make purchases, and calculate costs. Choose the unit that makes most sense for this ingredient.
fieldHelp.currentStock: The quantity you currently have in inventory right now. This will be measured in the unit you selected above.
fieldHelp.minimumStock: The absolute lowest quantity you want in stock. System triggers alerts when stock falls below this level.
fieldHelp.reorderPoint: The stock level that triggers a purchase order. Should be higher than minimum stock to account for delivery time. Example: If minimum is 5kg and delivery takes 3 days, set reorder point to 10kg.
fieldHelp.maximumStock: Maximum storage capacity for this ingredient. Helps prevent over-ordering and waste. Optional but recommended for perishable items.
fieldHelp.costPerUnit: The cost per unit of measurement you selected. For example, if you selected 'kg', enter the cost per kilogram. If 'liters', enter cost per liter.
fieldHelp.notifyOnLowStock: Sends email and in-app notifications when stock reaches the reorder point. The alert is still recorded on the dashboard even if disabled.
fieldHelp.perishable: Check this if the ingredient has an expiration date or limited shelf life (meat, dairy, produce). Enables expiration tracking and alerts.
fieldHelp.shelfLifeDays: The number of days this ingredient stays fresh after receiving it. This is used to automatically calculate expiration dates for each batch you receive. For example, if milk has a 7-day shelf life, a batch received today will expire in 7 days.

costCalculator.hint: Complete the 3 steps to calculate your cost per unit

step2.quantityTrackingDesc: For retail items (jewelry, clothing, etc.) - tracks quantity only

waste.insufficientStock: This waste log will result in negative stock. Stock cannot be negative.
waste.notesPlaceholder: Add any additional details about this waste...

SPOILED.label: Spoiled/Damaged
SPOILED.description: Product spoiled, moldy, or damaged before use

validation.confirmLargeAdjustmentMessage: This will change stock by {{amount}} {{unit}} ({{percentage}}% of current stock). Click Save again to confirm.
validation.largeAdjustmentWarning: This is a large adjustment ({{percentage}}% of current stock). Please verify the amount is correct.
validation.cannotReduceBelowZero: Cannot reduce stock below 0. Current stock is {{amount}} {{unit}}. Minimum adjustment: {{minimum}}
validation.negativeStockWarning: This adjustment will result in negative stock. Stock cannot be negative.
validation.optionalPriceFloor: Optional price floor - suggested price will not go below this value
validation.minimumStockMustBeLessOrEqualToReorderPo: Minimum stock must be less than or equal to reorder point. The reorder point is when a purchase order is triggered, so it should be higher than minimum stock to account for delivery time.

lowStockThreshold.label: Low Stock Alert Threshold
lowStockThreshold.title: What is Low Stock Threshold?
lowStockThreshold.description: Stock level that triggers a low stock alert in your dashboard. When stock falls below this number, the product will be highlighted.
lowStockThreshold.descriptionPortions: Number of portions that triggers a low stock alert. When available portions fall below this number, the product will be highlighted in your dashboard.
lowStockThreshold.defaultNote: Default: {{value}} units. Adjust based on your sales volume and reorder frequency.
lowStockThreshold.defaultNotePortions: Default: {{value}} portions. Adjust based on your daily sales and preparation time.
lowStockThreshold.separateFromReorder: Alert when stock falls below this level (separate from reorder point)
lowStockThreshold.alertWhenBelow: Alert when available portions fall below this level

discoveryBanner.title: 🎓 First time adding ingredients?
discoveryBanner.description: We walk you step by step through adding your first ingredient (milk, coffee, etc.).

discoveryBanner.title: 🎓 How to investigate what happened to your inventory?
discoveryBanner.description: History is your audit trail. We show you how to use filters to find any movement.

welcome.title: 📜 Movement history
welcome.description:
Every change to your inventory is recorded here: sales, purchases, adjustments, waste, theft, returns.

It's your audit trail: can't be edited or deleted. If anything doesn't add up, the answer is here.

step1.title: The table
step1.description:
Each row is a movement. Key columns:

• Date · when it happened
• Name · which item or ingredient
• Supplier · if involved
• Total cost · economic impact
• Adjustment · how many units added or subtracted

Click any row for full detail.

step2.title: Filters to investigate
step2.description:
When something doesn't add up, filter:

• Date · "What happened last Tuesday?"
• SKU · "What happened to this product?"
• Supplier · "What came from Acme Dairy?"
• Total cost · large movements
• Type · filter by movement class

step3.title: Type filter — the most powerful
step3.description:
Filter by movement type to spot patterns:

• Only sales → confirm POS deducted correctly
• Only manual adjustments → audit team decisions
• Only waste / theft / damage → compute monthly losses
• Only received purchases → tally inbound

complete.title: 🔍 Typical uses
complete.description:
• End of day: filter by date = today. Review suspicious moves.
• End of month: date + type = Loss/Damage/Theft. Compute waste.
• Count discrepancy: filter by SKU. Trace every in/out of that item.
• Team audit: filter by type = Manual adjustment. Review who did what.

discoveryBanner.title: 🎓 How to adjust stock when mercancía arrives or there's a shortage?
discoveryBanner.description: A step-by-step walkthrough — it's the most common day-to-day operation.

welcome.title: 📦 Adjusting stock
welcome.description:
Learn the day-to-day flow: receive mercancía, register a shortage, correct a theft, or record a physical count.

Every change is logged in History for audit.

step1.title: Click the stock number
step1.description: Each row shows current stock under Physical stock. Clicking that number opens a panel to adjust it.

step2.title: Pick the movement type
step2.description:
6 types. The rule is simple — pick the one that describes what actually happened:

• Stock received: arrived from supplier (adds)
• Inventory count: correct with physical count (sets total)
• Damage: broken/unusable product (subtracts)
• Theft: missing due to theft/loss (subtracts)
• Loss: expiration or waste (subtracts)
• Return: returned to supplier (subtracts)

step3.title: Enter the amount
step3.description:
For Stock received or Return: the amount adds or subtracts.
For Inventory count: enter the new total after the physical count — the system calculates the delta.

The New total shows automatically below.

step4.title: Save the adjustment
step4.description: When you save, stock updates and a permanent record appears in History with who, when and why. Audit trail is immutable.

complete.title: 🎉 That's it!
complete.description: Golden rule: always pick the right type. A mis-classified adjustment makes it impossible later to tell if it was theft, waste or error. When in doubt use Inventory count.

welcome.title: 🥛 Add an ingredient
welcome.description:
Ingredients are the inputs used in your recipes: milk, coffee, matcha, sugar, straws. They aren't sold on their own — they're consumed when preparing products.

Each ingredient must be created before it can be used in a recipe.

step1.title: "Add Ingredient" button
step1.description: Click here to open the form. You'll fill out a few basic fields.

step2.title: Ingredient name
step2.description: Use the name you'd call it in the kitchen. Example: Almond Milk, Espresso Coffee, Matcha Powder.

step3.title: Internal code (SKU)
step3.description: A unique code to identify it in reports. You can keep the one auto-generated or set your own (e.g. RM-MILK-ALM).

step4.title: Unit of measure
step4.description:
The unit you measure this ingredient in. Examples: kg for matcha, liters for milk, pieces for straws.

⚠️ Pick carefully — it can't easily be changed later.

step5.title: Category
step5.description: Group your ingredients for reports: Dairy, Protein, Beverages, Spices, etc.

step6.title: Current stock
step6.description:
Count how much you have RIGHT NOW in the chosen unit. Example: if you have 5 one-liter cartons of milk, enter 5.

Also set the minimum (when to alert) and reorder point (when to reorder).

step7.title: Cost per unit
step7.description: What you pay per unit to the supplier. Example: $45 per liter of milk. We use this to compute each product's cost and your Food Cost %.

step8.title: Save the ingredient
step8.description: Once saved, the ingredient is available for recipes. Repeat for each input you handle.

complete.title: 🎉 Done!
complete.description: Now that you have an ingredient, you can go to Products and create a recipe-based drink that uses it. Each sale will automatically deduct the quantity defined in the recipe.

discoveryBanner.title: 🎓 New to Avoqado? Take the complete walkthrough
discoveryBanner.description: A 3-minute tour of the whole inventory system: categories, ingredients, products, orders, stock, and history.

welcome.title: 🎓 Welcome to the inventory system
welcome.description:
This 3-minute tour teaches you the full system. By the end you'll know:

• What lives in each section
• When to use each one
• Where to start on day one

Every section then has its own deep-dive tour via the "?" button at the top right.

methods.title: 🔀 The 2 ways to track inventory
methods.description:
Every product can be tracked in two ways:

📦 By unit (QUANTITY): you count whole pieces.
Example: you sell hoodies or mugs — if you sell 1, stock drops by 1.

🥣 By recipe (RECIPE): the product is made from ingredients.
Example: a protein shake uses 120 ml milk + 30 g protein. On sale, ingredients are deducted, not the shake.

⚠️ Choose wisely: switching methods later is painful.

categories.title: 📂 Step 1 — Categories
categories.description:
Every product needs a category. This is the first step if you're starting from scratch.

Examples: Beverages, Food, Desserts, Merch, Shakes.

We recommend creating 3-5 categories before any products.

ingredients.title: 🥣 Step 2 — Ingredients (optional)
ingredients.description:
Ingredients (raw materials) are the inputs that feed your recipes.

Examples: milk, coffee beans, protein, ice, 12 oz cup, straw.

⚠️ Only needed if you use the RECIPE method. If all products are per-unit (hoodies, mugs), you can skip this step.

products.title: 📦 Step 3 — Products
products.description:
Products are what you actually sell. Here you pick the method:

• QUANTITY: "Black Hoodie M" — initial stock 20 pcs.
• RECIPE: "Chocolate Shake" — recipe of 120ml milk + 30g protein.

Each product belongs to a category (Step 1) and optionally has a SKU and barcode (GTIN).

purchaseOrders.title: 🛒 Step 4 — Purchase orders
purchaseOrders.description:
Purchase orders are how you request goods from suppliers. On receipt, the system creates batches with cost and expiry.

💡 Key: Without purchase orders, your recipe-based products don't know their cost — margins are wrong.

For per-unit (QUANTITY) products, purchase orders are optional but recommended for cost tracking.

stockSummary.title: 📊 Step 5 — Stock summary
stockSummary.description:
Your daily control panel. Shows:

• Current stock of every product/ingredient
• Low-stock alerts
• Manual adjustments (damage, theft, inventory count)

This is where you do day-to-day operations: "shipment arrived, fix this", "2 boxes broke", "we did a physical count".

history.title: 📋 Step 6 — History
history.description:
Every movement is logged forever: sales, adjustments, received orders. This is your audit trail.

Useful when inventory "doesn't match" and you need to investigate: who, when, what, and why.

⚠️ History is never deleted. If something was logged wrong, correct it with a new adjustment, not by deleting.

flow.title: 🔄 The full flow
flow.description:
This is how it all connects:

1️⃣ Create Categories
⬇️
2️⃣ (If using recipes) create Ingredients
⬇️
3️⃣ Create Products (pick QUANTITY or RECIPE)
⬇️
4️⃣ Place Purchase orders and receive them → batches with cost are created
⬇️
5️⃣ Sell on POS → stock drops automatically (unit or recipe)
⬇️
6️⃣ Manually adjust damage/loss in Stock summary
⬇️
7️⃣ Everything lands in History

complete.title: 🎉 You know the system!
complete.description:
To get started, follow this order:

✅ Categories → create 3-5
✅ Ingredients (only if you use recipes)
✅ Products → 5-10 to start
✅ Purchase order → receive initial stock
✅ Test sale → verify stock drops correctly

Every section has the "?" button with a deep-dive tour. If you get lost, come back here and replay this walkthrough.

Good luck!
